purpose: PreToolUse hook on Edit|Write. Blocks the master agent from writing to {project_root} during an active swarm (any non-ide dashboard with task != null). Reads project_root from .synapse/project.json; tracker_root from script location. Allow-list: tracker_root/{dashboards,tasks,Archive,.claude,.synapse}. If project_root == tracker_root (Synapse self-targeting), all writes allowed. If no swarm is active, all writes allowed (master can edit project files when not orchestrating). Otherwise: writes anywhere under project_root are blocked with `Master agent cannot write to project files during an active swarm. File: {path}. Create a worker task instead.`
gotchas: Active-swarm check iterates all non-ide dashboards looking for task!=null — if any one is active, the gate is on
Self-targeting bypass (PROJECT_ROOT == TRACKER_ROOT) — Synapse developing itself uses worker discipline anyway
Allow-list paths are ABSOLUTE matches against tracker_root prefix — symlinks may not be normalized
Fail-open if .synapse/project.json missing or project_root unset
patterns: Allow-list of permitted tracker subpaths + active-swarm gate on everything else
Absolute path resolution via cd-and-pwd with literal fallback
conventions: Block reason instructs the agent on the correct action ('Create a worker task instead')
